translated: That's right.
Looking at the illustrations, the core of Ena's journey...
is that she's been facing her art for years. It's not just that she likes it; she's suffered and struggled with many emotions while continuing to paint.
I think many of you found it easy to empathize with the moment in the story where a glimmer of hope finally appeared for her.
That's how I feel, anyway.
Now, since we have about four minutes left,
I'd like for us to take a look at the card illustrations together.
Before we look at the art, the illustration team, who understands Ena's feelings so well,
provided some comments about the costume Ena worked so hard for. Let me introduce those.
This time, it's about Ena's struggle to get into art school...
Saying she's "rewarded" might be too simple, but it's about her moving forward through that struggle.
Thank you very much.
The theme of the story was about facing a setback when her skills didn't reach the required level,
but still not giving up. When I first read the script, I really felt that core theme.
The design motif is a school uniform with a personal apron for painting. Congratulations to Suzuki for the production!
The design was made to look like an art student's style during their entrance exams.
As a style for Ena as a painter, it feels like she's finally made it this far.
While the outfit for this event is simple, the apron is made of patchwork.
This represents her standing tall and persevering even after nearly giving up many times.
It's a design with unique touches,
almost like things are finally coming together. Excuse me.
I need a lot of tissues... Here's the sailor collar.
They kept elements of the school uniform, like the collar and pleats, to leave a strong impression,
but added a tear-shaped arrangement.
They were very conscious of making it feel like a proper costume.
Seeing that in the illustration and the acting... it really comes through.
It really does.
Playing Ena... and then seeing the Training After version... the reaction from everyone was huge.
I was crying while recording,
and Ena is crying while she paints too.
Her hands are all dirty.
It's so moving.
And to all of you who watched over Ena and pressed the button to support her,
helping her move forward to the next step...
The time leading up to that button press, both before and after training, is truly precious. Thank you for everything.
I'm just so grateful and moved. As someone on the creative side,
I had so many thoughts about this story.
Even though the genre is different, I felt I could really understand Ena's feelings.
And Kaito... Kaito-san gave Ena so many important words.
They even eat ice cream together, so please check that out.
I called it "Yogurt Ice Cream" in my head.
That's because I personally love yogurt ice cream.
So...
It's like, "Wait, it's just your preference?"
And then there's Miku-san.
What a lovely picture.
She's eating ice cream too.
So cute.
And Mafuyu...
If you long-press...
She's holding an apron.
Thank you very much.
So, we've looked at the illustrations for before and after training.
Oh man, how many times have I done this now?
Thank you for taking a look.
I can talk about the story for a few more minutes, so I will.
Regarding the previous story... well,
how should I put it?
It wasn't specifically about her art.
Before that, I sang the song "I am Rain."
When we had a story about Ena's art—I don't like saying "drawings"—
when we had a story about her art, I happened to go to a museum.
I was able to act while keeping in mind what it means to look at a painting.
To get to know Ena better... this time, I happened to be walking down a street,
and there was a school that people aiming for art school probably attend.
I thought, "So this is what it's like."
Even just looking from the outside, I felt a great sense of tension. I recorded my lines while realizing how tough of an environment she's in.
I try to act every day in a way that conveys the atmosphere of the place where Ena is to all of you.
Thank you very much.
So, we have a few dozen seconds left.
By now, some of you might have already seen the AfterLive.
Since it takes place after the story,
you can see Ena's refreshed face. Things will still be hard from here on out,
but you can see in her expression that she's truly made her decision.
